top_findings.summary: The skill requests broad file system access via globs '**/*.*' (all files in all directories) and command execution via the Bash tool, which are excessive for its primary purpose of reviewing and ensuring latest language versions and best practices in code. This could allow unintended access to sensitive non-code files (e.g., configuration files, logs, personal data) and arbitrary command execution beyond necessary code analysis or refactoring tasks, violating data minimization principles. The skill should limit access to specific code file extensions or explicitly justify the need for such wide permissions.
